revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jikkō kyō]][[ja:実行教]]




 


{{AfC submission|t||ts=20230320105014|u=Immanuelle|ns=118|demo=}}

{{notability|date=March 2023}}

{{nihongo|'''Jikkō kyō'''|実行教}} is a [[Sect Shinto]] organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Japanese Meaning of 実行教, じっこうきょう, jikkōkyō |url=https://www.nihongomaster.com/japanese/dictionary/word/118012/jikkoukyou-%E5%AE%9F%E8%A1%8C%E6%95%99-%E3%81%98%E3%81%A3%E3%81%93%E3%81%86%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86 |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Nihongo Master |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /> and one of the thirteen shinto sects.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=井上 |first=順孝 |title= |date=April 1991 |publisher=弘文堂 |isbn=978-4335160219 |page=11 |script-title=sn:教派神道の形成 |author-link=井上順孝}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

It had a very complex institutional history having been formed in the {{ill|16th century|en|16th century|qid=Q7017|lt=16th century|1=16th century}} by {{ill|Hasegawa Kakugyo|en|Hasegawa Kakugyo|qid=Q11630860|lt=Hasegawa Kakugyo|1=Hasegawa Kakugyo}} (who was also associated with [[Fuso-kyo]]) and eventually moved to support the Meiji government<ref>{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=xlG3F6gSm9UC&dq=Jikk%C5%8D+ky%C5%8D&pg=PA131 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |isbn=978-0-8108-7372-8 |language=en}}</ref>

It is considered a "{{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|qid=Q93697439|lt=Mountain worship|1=Mountain worship}} sect" alongside [[Fuso-kyo]]<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kyōha Shintō {{!}} Japanese religion |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Kyoha-Shinto |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |language=en}}</ref>.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Shintō - Shintō reaction against Buddhism {{!}} Britannica |url=https://www.britannica.com/topic/Shinto/Shinto-reaction-against-Buddhism |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=www.britannica.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=History of Art: Visual History of the World- Shinto |url=http://www.all-art.org/Visual_History/21-5.htm |access-date=2023-03-06 |website=www.all-art.org}}</ref> and {{ill|Ontake-kyō|en|Ontake-kyō|qid=Q6880734|lt=Ontakekyō|1=Ontake-kyō}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=What is sectarian Shinto? - The Handy Religion Answer Book |url=https://www.papertrell.com/apps/preview/The-Handy-Religion-Answer-Book/Handy%20Answer%20book/What-is-sectarian-Shinto/001137024/content/SC/52cb017682fad14abfa5c2e0_default.html |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=www.papertrell.com}}</ref>

==Overview==

{{Infobox organization
| Name         = Jikkōkyō (實行教)
| image        = [[File:Jikkokyo Headquarters.JPG|280px]]
| image_caption = Jikkōkyō Headquarters
| Map          = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|55|33.1|N|139|37|39.1|E}}}}
| Established  = 1878 (Meiji 11)
| Type         = {{ill|Religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious corporation|qq=}}
| Headquarters = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|qid=Q134635|lt=Saitama City|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|北區|en|北區|qid=Q1180096|lt=Kita-ku, Saitama|1=北區}}, {{ill|Ōmiya Bonsai Village|en|Ōmiya Bonsai Village|qid=Q8081624|lt=Ōmiya Bonsai Village|1=Ōmiya Bonsai Village}} 377
| Founder      = {{ill|Shibata Hanamori|ja|柴田花守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Hanamori|1=Shibata Hanamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134509164}}
| Leader_title = Dōshu (道主)
| Leader_name  = {{ill|Shibata Hiroyuki|ja|柴田尋之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Hiroyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
}}

'''Jikkōkyō''' (實行教) is a sect of {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} and part of {{ill|Sect Shinto|en|Sect Shinto|qid=Q1532150|lt=Sect Shinto|1=Sect Shinto}}, originating from the {{ill|Fujidō|ja|不二道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小谷三志|1=Sanshi Otani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209484}}, one of the many {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|qid=Q11456201|lt=Fujikō|1=Fujiko (religion)}} organizations dedicated to {{ill|Mount Fuji faith|ja|富士信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji faith|1=Fujisan Sengen Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455920}}. The official legal name uses the traditional characters (實行教), but many affiliated religious corporations are registered under the modern spelling (実行教).

== Overview ==

Jikkōkyō was founded by {{ill|Shibata Hanamori|ja|柴田花守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Hanamori|1=Shibata Hanamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134509164}} (also known as Sakyuki) in {{ill|1878|en|1878|qid=Q7776|lt=1878|1=1878}} (Meiji 11) as "Jikkōsha" (実行社). It became an independent sect of {{ill|Sect Shinto|en|Sect Shinto|qid=Q1532150|lt=Kōha Shintō|1=Sect Shinto}} in {{ill|1882|en|1882|qid=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) and was officially recognized as "Jikkōkyō"{{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}.

Like {{ill|Fusōkyō|ja|扶桑教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusōkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fuso-kyo|qid=Q11497295}}, Jikkōkyō traces its lineage to {{ill|Hasegawa Kakugyō|ja|長谷川角行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasegawa Kakugyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 角行 (富士講)|1=Hasegawa Kakugyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630860}}, the founder of the Fuji faith. It follows the branch of {{ill|Itō Miroku|ja|伊藤身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Miroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as {{ill|Shokugyō Miroku|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokugyō Miroku|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}}
{{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka|1991|p=17}}.

Jikkōkyō descends through:
- '''Fifth-generation leader''' {{ill|Shokugyō Miroku|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokugyō Miroku|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}}
- '''Sixth-generation leader''' {{ill|Itō Ikko|ja|伊藤一行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Ikko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third daughter of Miroku
- '''Seventh-generation leader''' {{ill|Itō Sango|ja|伊藤参行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Sango|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ikko’s adopted son

{{ill|Itō Sango|ja|伊藤参行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Sango|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attracted disciples, including {{ill|Kotani Sanshi|ja|小谷三志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Sanshi|1=Sanshi Otani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209484}}, who expanded the sect{{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka|1991|p=17}}. In {{ill|1838|en|1838|qid=Q7612|lt=1838|1=1838}} (Tenpō 9), the sect was renamed '''Fujidō''' (不二道){{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka|1991|p=18}}. 

Two major branches emerged:
1. '''Fusōkyō''' (spread by the Shishino family)
2. '''Jikkōkyō''' (spread by the Shibata family)<ref>[https://books.google.co.jp/books?id=3418dDYaP3IC&pg=PA190　 『国家に及ぼす宗教の害毒』日高瓊々彦、大同館書店, 1928]</ref>

Jikkōkyō emphasizes '''practical action and discipline''' over Fuji faith’s esoteric aspects{{sfn|Sugata|1985|p=167}}. Members receive '''a spiritual name''' upon initiation, indicating their training path, and later, '''three titles of achievement''' upon reaching higher spiritual progress.

== Lineage of Successive Leaders ==

{{See also|Fujikō#List of major leaders}}

* '''Founder of Fujidō''': {{ill|Hasegawa Kakugyō|ja|長谷川角行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasegawa Kakugyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 角行 (富士講)|1=Hasegawa Kakugyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630860}}
* '''Second-generation leader''': {{ill|Kurono Nikkō Nichigan|ja|黒野日行日玵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurono Nikkō Nichigan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''Third-generation leader''': {{ill|Akaha Ryōgyō Ganshin|ja|赤葉澰行玵心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akaha Ryōgyō Ganshin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''Fourth-generation leader''': {{ill|Mori Getsugyō Kaichō|ja|森月行劊忡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Getsugyō Kaichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''Fifth-generation leader''': {{ill|Shokugyō Miroku|ja|食行身禄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokugyō Miroku|1=Jikigyō Miroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666087}} (Itō Miroku)
* '''Sixth-generation leader''': {{ill|Itō Ikko|ja|伊藤一行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Ikko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichihime, third daughter of Miroku)
* '''Seventh-generation leader''': {{ill|Itō Sango|ja|伊藤参行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Sango|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Itō Sango Rokuō)
* '''Eighth-generation leader''': {{ill|Kotani Sanshi|ja|小谷三志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Sanshi|1=Sanshi Otani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209484}} (Kotani Roku Gyō Sanshi)
* '''Ninth-generation leader''': {{ill|Tokudaiji San Gyō San Soku|ja|徳大寺参行三息|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokudaiji San Gyō San Soku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''Tenth-generation leader (First Head of Jikkōkyō)''': {{ill|Shibata Sakyuki Sansei|ja|柴田咲行三生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Sakyuki Sansei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibata Hanamori){{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}
* '''Eleventh-generation leader''': {{ill|Udono Chūgyō San Shin|ja|鵜殿中行三親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udono Chūgyō San Shin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Posthumously honored){{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}
* '''Twelfth-generation leader (Second Head of Jikkōkyō)''': {{ill|Shibata Chūgyō San Jo|ja|柴田忠行三恕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Chūgyō San Jo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}
* '''Thirteenth-generation leader (Third Head of Jikkōkyō)''': {{ill|Shibata Chūgyō San Shu|ja|柴田中行三守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Chūgyō San Shu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibata Montarō){{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}
* '''Fourteenth-generation leader (Fourth Head of Jikkōkyō)''': {{ill|Shibata Kingyō San Bi|ja|柴田謹行三美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Kingyō San Bi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibata Dōshu){{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}
* '''Fifteenth-generation leader (Fifth Head of Jikkōkyō)''': {{ill|Shibata Tsingyō San Rei|ja|柴田辰行三禮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Tsingyō San Rei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibata Tatsuhiko){{sfn|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|1996|p=98}}
* '''Sixteenth-generation leader (Sixth Head of Jikkōkyō)''': {{ill|Shibata Hiroyuki|ja|柴田尋之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Hiroyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==

* {{Cite book|和書|author={{ill|Inoue Nobutaka|en|Inoue Nobutaka|qid=Q969889|lt=Inoue Nobutaka|1=Inoue Nobutaka}}|title={{ill|Formation of Kōha Shintō|ja|教派神道の形成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Formation of Kōha Shintō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579532|1=Q135579532}}|publisher={{ill|Kōbundō|ja|弘文堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbundō|1=Kōbundō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487344}}|date=1991-04|isbn=978-4335160219|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|author={{ill|Inoue Nobutaka et al.|ja|井上順孝ほか編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Nobutaka et al.|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|title={{ill|New Religious Organizations and Leaders Encyclopedia|ja|新宗教教団・人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Religious Organizations and Leaders Encyclopedia|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579533|1=New Religious Organizations and Leaders Encyclopedia}}|publisher=Kōbundō|date=1996-01|isbn=978-4335160288|ref=harv}}
* {{Cite book|和書|last=Sugata|first=Masaaki|authorlink={{ill|Sugata Masaaki|ja|菅田正昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata Masaaki|1=Masaaki Sugata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619352}}|year=1985|title={{ill|The Revival of Koshintō|ja|古神道は甦る|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Revival of Koshintō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tama Publishing|ja|たま出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Publishing|1=Tama Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11271379}}|isbn=4884811321|ref=harv}}
